relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The disciples admire temple stones and buildings.
Jesus says the great structure will be thrown down.
On the Mount of Olives, the inner four ask privately.
They ask for timing and sign.
The first warning is not to be led astray.
Many claims will come wearing his name.
Wars and reports are not yet the end.
The shaking of nations is called birth-pains.
Witness will happen under pressure.
The good message moves toward all nations.
In the hour of handing-over, holy Spirit gives speech.
Even family bonds can fracture under crisis.
Endurance carries the path through hatred.
The desolating sign calls for urgent discernment.
There is no time to retrieve possessions.
The field worker must not turn back.
The vulnerable are named with grief.
Prayer enters even the season of flight.
The pressure is described as creation-deep crisis.
The days are shortened for the chosen.
Do not trust every claim of Messiah’s location.
Signs and wonders can still mislead.
Watch, because he has told them beforehand.
Cosmic lights darken after the pressure.
Heavenly powers are shaken.
The Son of Humanity comes in clouds with power and glory.
The chosen are gathered from earth and heaven’s edges.
The fig tree teaches discernment of nearness.
When these things happen, the door is near.
This generation is placed inside the crisis.
His words outlast heaven and earth.
The day and hour belong to the Father’s knowing.
The command is watchfulness.
The servants receive work while the master journeys.
The doorkeeper must watch through every watch of the night.
Sudden arrival must not find them asleep.
The word to all is simple: Keep watch.
